question: Comment router les taches d'ingenierie logicielle entre agent-autonome, agent-propose et humain-led selon le type de tache ?

principles.text: Etablir une policy explicite mappant les classes de taches a leur niveau d'autonomie. Agent autonome acceptable : test scaffolding, renames, dependency bumps, low/medium complexity bug fixes (SWE-bench-like). Agent propose (humain approuve) : refactors multi-fichiers, nouveaux endpoints. Humain-led obligatoire : architecture, schema migrations, security-sensitive code, data migrations, force-push main.

sources.name: SWE-Bench Pro arXiv:2509.16941 - Long-Horizon SE Tasks benchmark
sources.quote: OpenAI GPT-5 and Claude Opus 4.1 achieve the highest resolve rates at 23.3% and 22.7% respectively

sources.name: GitHub Copilot coding agent documentation
sources.quote: the agent excels at low-to-medium complexity tasks in well-tested codebases, from adding features and fixing bugs to extending tests, refactoring code, and improving documentation

sources.name: Toth, Feng et al. arXiv:2509.14745
sources.quote: revisions to Agentic-PRs target bug fixes (45.1%), documentation updates (27.4%), refactoring (25.7%), and code style improvements (22.1%)

sources.name: Hou et al. 2024 - LLMs for Software Engineering: A Systematic Literature Review
sources.quote: 395 research papers from January 2017 to January 2024 to answer four key research questions

minus: indirectness (benchmarks not production routing), -1 compromise divergence

etd_balance.benefices: +taux succes (SWE-Bench Pro delta ~3x inter-types), cout optimise par classe
etd_balance.risques: Complexite infra routing, mauvaise classification initiale
